senses.definition: a player in a sport, particularly in soccer or basketball, who primarily focuses on scoring goals or points
the three central positions on an American football team's offensive line, typically including the left guard, center, and right guard

examples.x: The coach emphasized the importance of the interior offensive line in protecting the quarterback.
The team's success relied heavily on the strength of their interior offensive line.
Injuries to the interior offensive line affected the running game's efficiency.
The interior offensive line opened up significant running lanes for the tailback.
During the draft, the team prioritized bolstering their interior offensive line.

senses.definition: an American football position in the offensive backfield, typically responsible for carrying the ball on running plays and catching passes

examples.x: The team's offense relies heavily on the tailback for gaining yardage on the ground.
The coach emphasized the importance of the tailback in both running and passing plays.
Blocking assignments for the tailback are crucial in protecting the quarterback.
Playing the tailback requires a combination of vision, strength, and quickness.
The team's strategy includes using the tailback as a receiver out of the backfield.

senses.definition: a versatile offensive American football position that combines the roles of a fullback and a tight end
a position in American football for a wide receiver who lines up on the line of scrimmage but farthest from the center

examples.x: Playing the split end requires exceptional speed and agility.
The split end is positioned farthest from the center on the line of scrimmage.
Success at the split end demands precise route-running skills.
The split end is crucial for spreading the defense and creating passing lanes.
Being a split end involves beating the cornerback in one-on-one matchups.

senses.definition: a position in American football that lines up near the offensive line and serves as both a receiver and a blocker

examples.x: The tight end's placement on the field allows for versatile offensive plays.
Being a tight end involves both protecting the quarterback and running routes.
Playing the tight end requires a combination of blocking and receiving skills.
Success at the tight end demands physical strength and catching ability.
Playing the tight end effectively can greatly enhance the offense's flexibility.

senses.definition: a key position in rugby responsible for directing the attack and often acting as the team's playmaker

examples.x: Our fly-half is crucial for setting up offensive plays.
He was known as the best fly-half in the league.
He started playing rugby as a fly-half but later switched to inside centre.

senses.definition: a position in American football responsible for stopping the run and pressuring the quarterback
a baseball position located between second and third base, responsible for fielding ground balls

examples.x: Playing shortstop requires quick reflexes and strong defensive skills.
The shortstop is crucial for turning double plays.
Being a shortstop involves both catching line drives and fielding ground balls.
Playing shortstop effectively can significantly strengthen the team's defense.

senses.definition: a defensive position in soccer or American football, where a player guards the central area near their team's goal

examples.x: She has been playing center back since she joined the team.
Our coach prefers a tall and agile player at center back.
He moved from midfield to center back to strengthen the defense.
His speed and anticipation make him a formidable center back.
She has been praised for her composure and leadership as the team's center back.

senses.definition: a position in basketball or hockey, where players use their size and strength near the opponent's goal

examples.x: The hockey team's power forward is known for crashing the net and creating scoring opportunities.
She's a versatile player who can switch between small forward and power forward positions.
The power forward's role includes setting screens and defending against opponents' big men.
His speed and agility make him a formidable power forward on the ice.
The power forward position requires both finesse and physicality.

senses.definition: a position in basketball responsible for playing on the perimeter, scoring, rebounding, and defending

examples.x: She plays small forward for her college basketball team.
He was recruited for his shooting skills as a small forward.
His versatility allows him to play small forward and power forward interchangeably.
She aspires to become a professional small forward in the WNBA.
The team's small forward contributed with key steals in the final minutes.

senses.definition: a position in basketball responsible for bringing the ball up the court and initiating the team's offense

examples.x: She's known for her quick reflexes and sharp passes as a point guard.
The team relies on their point guard to control the tempo of the game.
His height is not typical for a point guard, but his speed and court vision compensate for it well.
He transitioned from shooting guard to point guard this season.
The coach emphasized the importance of decision-making for the point guard.

senses.definition: a position in basketball that is in charge of scoring points by shooting the ball, typically from long range

examples.x: She plays as a shooting guard for her college basketball team.
He was drafted into the NBA as a shooting guard.
The shooting guard is known for their three-point shooting ability.
She transitioned from small forward to shooting guard this season.
She has been training hard to become a more consistent shooting guard.

senses.definition: a position in team sports like soccer or field hockey, where the player's primary role is to score goals and create opportunities for teammates
a football player who runs, blocks, or catches the ball

examples.x: As a back, he was known for his ability to evade tacklers.
A strong back is essential for a team’s rushing offense.
The back caught a short pass and turned it into a big play.

senses.definition: (American football) a position that leads the offense, responsible for calling plays, handling the snap, passing the ball

examples.x: Playing the quarterback requires leadership and excellent passing skills.
The quarterback is positioned behind the center at the start of the play.
The quarterback is crucial for directing the offense and calling plays.
Coaches rely on the player playing the quarterback to read the defense and adjust the play.
Playing the quarterback effectively can significantly impact the team's performance.

senses.definition: a rugby position, typically involving tall, powerful players who specialize in line-outs and scrums

examples.x: The coach praised the lock forward for his defensive skills.
Our team's lock forward dominated the scrums.
Injuries forced the lock forward to leave the game early.
During the scrum, the lock forward provided immense strength.
The lock forward jumped high to win the ball.

senses.definition: a fielding position in cricket close to the batsman on the off side, between the slips and point

examples.x: The fielding team set an attacking field with two slips and a gully for the new batsman.
The batsman edged the ball straight to gully, where it was safely caught.

senses.definition: a fielding position in cricket close to the wicket-keeper, typically used for catching edges from the batsman

examples.x: The captain positioned his best fielder at first slip for the new ball.
The ball flew off the edge and straight into the hands of the slip.
The slip was alert and took three catches in the session.
The batsman edged it, but it fell short of the slip.
The fast bowler induced an edge, and it went straight to second slip.

senses.definition: a fielding position on the cricket field, positioned straight ahead and closer to the bowler than the batsman on the off side

examples.x: Success at mid-on demands good fielding technique and agile movement.
Being at mid-on involves covering ground quickly and diving to stop the ball.
Teams depend on the player at mid-on to maintain pressure on the batsmen.
Playing mid-on requires keen anticipation and quick reflexes.
Fielding at mid-on effectively can save crucial runs and create opportunities for dismissals.

senses.definition: a fielding position in cricket on the boundary behind the bowler's arm, toward the leg side

examples.x: He hit the ball straight down to long on for a single.
The fielder stationed at long on took a brilliant catch.
The captain placed his best fielder at long on for the last over.
The bowler bowled a slower ball, deceiving the batsman at long on.
The fielder at long on had to run in quickly to cut off the boundary.

senses.definition: the fielding position in cricket deep on the off side, typically towards the boundary

examples.x: The captain placed a fielder at long off to prevent easy singles.
The fielder stationed at long off took a brilliant catch near the boundary.
The batsman lofted the ball straight down to long off for a single.
The bowler bowled a full delivery and the batsman drove it to long off for a boundary.
The team's strategy involved keeping long off and long on deep to restrict big hits.

senses.definition: a fielding position in cricket placed behind square leg on the boundary
a fielding position in cricket positioned behind the batsman on the leg side, typically at an acute angle to the batsman's stance
a fielding position in cricket roughly square of the batsman on the leg side

examples.x: The fielder stationed at square leg took a sharp catch.
The ball was flicked beautifully off the pads through square leg for four runs.
The batsman tried to sweep but mistimed it, hitting the ball straight to square leg.
The fielder at square leg was quick to cut off the boundary, saving two runs.
The captain positioned his best fielder at square leg for the spinner's next over.

senses.definition: (baseball) a position in the outfield responsible for covering a significant portion of the outfield and catching balls hit in the center

examples.x: Playing center field requires exceptional speed and good judgment.
The center field is positioned deep in the outfield to cover a wide range of hits.
Success in center field demands strong fielding skills and accurate throwing.
The center field is crucial for tracking down long fly balls and line drives.
Being in center field involves reading the trajectory of the ball and making timely catches.

senses.definition: a baseball position in the outfield responsible for covering the area between center field and the right foul line

examples.x: Playing right field requires a strong throwing arm and good fielding skills.
Being in right field involves anticipating the trajectory of fly balls and line drives.
The right field is essential for preventing extra bases and making accurate throws to bases.
Success in right field demands quick reaction times and accurate throws to bases.
Fielding effectively in right field can change the momentum of the game and secure victories for the team.

senses.definition: a baseball fielding position in the infield responsible for fielding ground balls and covering the base on stolen base attempts

examples.x: Playing second base requires agility and quick reflexes.
Success at second base demands strong fielding and throwing skills.
Playing second base effectively can significantly impact the team's defense and overall performance.
Being at second base involves anticipating plays and reacting swiftly to ground balls.
The second base is crucial for turning double plays and fielding bunts.

senses.definition: (baseball) a position in the outfield responsible for covering the area between center field and the left foul line

examples.x: Success in left field demands quick reflexes and the ability to throw accurately to bases.
Being in left field involves anticipating the trajectory of the ball and making decisive catches.
Excelling in left field can impact the game by limiting runs and contributing to defensive strategies effectively.
Teams depend on the player at left field ability to read the game and adjust positioning as needed.
Playing left field requires good defensive skills and the ability to judge fly balls accurately.

senses.definition: a fielding position in the infield responsible for fielding ground balls, making throws to bases, and defending against bunts

examples.x: Being at third base involves anticipating plays and positioning oneself to field ground balls.
The player at third base needs to have good instincts for reacting to baserunners and making split-second decisions.
Success at third base demands accurate throws and the ability to read batted balls.
Playing third base requires quick reflexes and strong defensive skills.
Playing third base effectively can turn potential hits into outs and impact the outcome of close games.

senses.definition: the position of an offensive lineman in American football who is responsible for snapping the ball to the quarterback and blocking defenders

examples.x: So this season, is she on the bench or is she playing center?
The player who was playing the center blocked the defenders effectively.
He was praised for the role he had in winning since he covered the center that night.
Playing the center, he directs the offensive line and adjusts protections based on defensive alignments.
She told the coach that she would make a good candidate for center in the team.

senses.definition: the position of a basketball player who typically plays near the basket, responsible for scoring close-range shots

examples.x: She played the center in last year's match.
When playing the center, he focuses on scoring in the post.
Are you playing the center tonight?
The tallest person on the basketball team gets the center.
I thought that they picked the right person for the center this season.

senses.definition: a position in hockey played by a forward who is placed in the middle of the ice, responsible for taking faceoffs, setting up plays, and scoring goals

examples.x: The coach selected the player for center very carefully.
She scored a goal in her first time playing the center.
Being at center in hockey involves scoring goals.
The team's player at center is skilled in distributing the puck to teammates.
Playing the center, he had offensive strategies and provided support in both zones.
